summary: PR #2338 was a closed, unmerged visual-effects decomp/naming slice focused on src/melee/ef. It renamed/reorganized ef-related files and symbols, added descriptive names for many efLib/efAsync/efAlt functions and data, moved efdata/efalt into matching config, and improved several ef units according to the bot report. The author explicitly marked several naming and split decisions as provisional and noted open jump-table matching questions for efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn.

key_files.role: Main async effect implementation work; efAsync_Dispatch and efAsync_Spawn were called out in PR body/bot report.
evidence_refs: pr_body: did work on async; efAsync_Dispatch TODO at 98%
bot_report: efAsync_Spawn 99.88% to 100%; efAsync_Dispatch 98.46% to 98.60%; efasync .data regression

evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/db/dballoc.c, ftCo_Guard.c, ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftdata.c, ftdemo.c, ftlib.c, gm_16AE.c, gm_1832.c, gmregtyfall.c, gmstaffroll.c, lbdvd.c, vi0102.c, vi0401.c, vi0402.c, vi0501.c, vi0502.c, vi0601.c, vi0801.c, vi1201v1.c, vi1202.c, src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h

what_changed: Replaced efSpecial_SpawnSpecial/efspecial split naming with efAlt_Spawn/efalt in symbols, splits, configure.py, and source/header files.
Moved the old eflib_alloc split/build entry toward efdata and added efdata.c/efdata.h.

Renamed many address-style efLib and efAsync function symbols to descriptive names such as efLib_Init, efLib_Create, efLib_CreateGenerator_*, efLib_Cb_*, efAsync_Dispatch, efAsync_LoadAsync, efAsync_QueueFlush, and efAsync_QueueInit.
Renamed several ef data/bss/sbss symbols, including efAsync_DatEntries, efLib_AnimQueue, efLib_ParamTable, efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, and efLib_AnimCount.
Touched ef headers/types/forward declarations and small callsites across fighter, item, game-mode, stage, vi, lb, and sysdolphin baselib files.
Added M_PI_F/M_PI_L and related PI constants to src/MSL/math.h.
Bot report showed net matched-code improvement of +656 bytes, 9 new matches, 6 broken matches associated with renamed/moved units, 2 unmatched-item improvements, and one efasync .data regression.

smart_moves.text: The PR author explicitly marked descriptive ef names as provisional and invited better names, reducing the risk of treating speculative names as final.
evidence_refs: pr_body: I am not attached to any of the names I am using here

smart_moves.text: The PR body recorded exact unresolved match percentages and jump-table concerns for efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn, making future follow-up searchable.
evidence_refs: pr_body: efAsync_Dispatch 98%, jump table 53%, second jump table 88%

smart_moves.text: The author separated likely temporary file-split naming from matching progress, noting possible future adjustment if ASM string evidence indicates only eflib.c and efasync.c existed.
evidence_refs: pr_body: looked at strings in the ASM; think there was only ever eflib.c and efasync.c; splits/symbols should be adjusted accordingly with time

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a PR renames or moves a matched translation unit, bot reports may show paired new matches and broken matches for old unit names; intake should preserve that as a rename/split effect rather than assuming a true source regression without more evidence.
evidence_refs: bot_report: efalt items new 100%; efspecial items broken 100% to 0%; efdata .bss/.sbss new; eflib_alloc .bss broken

decomp_lessons.lesson: Jump-table-heavy dispatch/spawn functions should keep unresolved matching strategy explicit: the PR records uncertainty between hardcoding a jump table and restructuring source or representing it as .data.
evidence_refs: pr_body: efSync_Spawn associated jump table TODO

decomp_lessons.lesson: Descriptive renames are useful for review/search but should remain conservative when the author says they are not attached to the names and no reviewer acceptance is present.
evidence_refs: pr_body: gave descriptive naming to ef-related functions
pr_body: not attached to any names

naming_conventions.pattern: efLib_* for visual-effect library functions, with descriptive verbs/nouns such as Init, SetFlags, Destroy, Create, CreateGenerator, SpawnParticleEffect, SetParamAlpha, SetParamGfxId.
efAsync_* for async effect load/queue functions.
efAlt_* was used in this slice for the function/file formerly represented as efSpecial_*; author said efalt may be renamed back to special later.
evidence_refs: pr_body: efalt was named special after original MR; feel free to rename it back to special

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use bot/checkdiff percentages to prioritize remaining work: efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn were close text matches but still had poor jump-table/data matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No build log or objdiff details beyond bot report were loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Consider source restructuring before hardcoding jump tables; hardcoding was mentioned only as an unresolved possibility, not an accepted tactic.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Unsure if we should hard code the jump table or get it matching by properly structuring things
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is an open follow-up, not evidence that either strategy was attempted successfully.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use ASM string evidence as a potential signal for translation-unit split ownership.
evidence_refs: pr_body: looked at strings in the ASM; think there was only ever eflib.c and efasync.c
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The author expressed uncertainty with '(?)' and deferred split/symbol adjustment.

review_feedback.text: Report showed matched code 59.22% (+0.02%, +656 bytes), total data -13 bytes, matched data +27 bytes, and linked data -4 bytes.
Nine new matches included efAlt_Spawn, efalt data/sdata/sdata2, efdata bss/sbss, efsync sdata, efAsync_Spawn, and efasync sdata.
Six broken matches were reported for old efspecial, eflib_alloc, and eflib sbss units after renaming/split movement.
Unmatched improvements were efSync_Spawn and efAsync_Dispatch; efasync .data regressed from 65.93% to 24.82%.

ef folder ASM strings only eflib.c efasync.c splits symbols

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat moved/renamed matched units carefully in postmortems
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2338 shows a rename/split move where efalt/efdata became new matches while efspecial/eflib_alloc appeared as broken matches in the automated report. For historical intake, preserve both sides and avoid labeling the old-unit breakage as an ordinary regression without additional evidence.
evidence_refs: diff: efspecial.c to efalt.c; eflib_alloc.c to efdata.c
bot_report: new matches and broken matches tables

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record unresolved jump-table strategy explicitly
accepted_candidate_records.text: For near-matching dispatch/spawn functions, PR #2338 preserved the uncertainty between hardcoding jump tables, restructuring source, or matching a data-located jump table. This is useful follow-up metadata but not proof that any tactic is accepted.

source_update_candidates.title: Possible original ef translation-unit split may be only eflib.c and efasync.c
source_update_candidates.text: Author noted that ASM strings suggested there may only ever have been eflib.c and efasync.c in this folder, and that files/splits/symbols should be adjusted with time. The note is uncertain and should be verified before promoting.
evidence_refs: pr_body: I looked at the strings in the ASM, I think there was only ever eflib.c and efasync.c (?)
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential TU ownership/split fact, but stated with uncertainty and not validated in loaded evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: efalt/efspecial naming is unresolved for gfx_id range 0x478..0x4BA
source_update_candidates.text: Author stated efalt was named special after an original MR and may be renamed back to special. They also noted it is only called if efSync_Spawn is called with 0x478 >= gfx_id < 0x4BA, preserving the exact text despite the unusual inequality.
evidence_refs: pr_body: efalt was named special after my original MR; feel free to rename it back to special
pr_body: only ever called if efSync_Spawn is called with 0x478 >= gfx_id < 0x4BA
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible naming/path fact and call-range clue, but author framed the name as provisional and the loaded evidence does not validate the range.

source_update_candidates.title: Particle linkNo skip/render masks need canonical home
source_update_candidates.text: PR body included temporary particle.h notes for PTCL_SKIP_LINKNO_0/1/2 bits 16+ and PTCL_RENDER_LINKNO_0/1/2 bits 0+, tied to hsd_8039CEAC, hsd_8039EE24, and psDispParticles. Author marked these as TODO until particle headers are fleshed out.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Notes about particle.h temp only used in ef
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data/header naming documentation update, but explicitly temporary and not yet canonical.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat hardcoded jump tables as accepted without evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR only records hardcoding jump tables as an unresolved question for efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn. Any broader standard should say hardcoded jump tables require explicit evidence and review, not that this PR endorsed the tactic.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Unsure if we should hard code the jump table or get it matching by properly structuring things
pr_body: Unsure if we should hardcode it or try to match it as a .data-located variable
source_update_candidates.reason: Could reinforce existing matching-tactics caution, but loaded evidence is an uncertainty note rather than a successful tactic.

rejection_notes: Do not promote efAlt naming as canonical; the author explicitly said the name may be changed back to special.
Do not promote the descriptive efLib/efAsync names as accepted global naming standards; no human review acceptance is loaded.
Do not claim efAsync_Dispatch or efSync_Spawn matched; loaded evidence says they remained around 98%.
Do not claim the PR was merged; metadata says state closed and merged_at null.
Do not treat the particle linkNo mask definitions as canonical; the PR body says they are temporary until particle headers are fleshed out.
